name: Castillo de Irulegui – circular por la Sierra de Tajonar y Balsa Eskoriz
region_intro: Mountain biking around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) offers diverse terrain across its natural features. The municipality is characterized by mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Tajonar and Sierra de Alaiz, providing varied inclines and descents. The Elorz River traverses the valley, contributing to lush landscapes and riverside paths. Ancient forests, including beech, oak, and kermes oak groves, offer shaded trails and a vibrant natural environment for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) The most popular mountain bike trail is Castillo de Irulegui – circular por la Sierra de Tajonar y Balsa Eskoriz, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Tajonar. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ezkoriz Pond – View of the Elorz Valley loop from Noain-Aeropuerto / Aireportua, a moderate 20.7 miles (33.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Elorz Valley and passes by the Ezkoriz Pond. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Elorz Valley loop from Noain-Aeropuerto / Aireportua, a 27.4 miles (44.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Elorz Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 4 easy, 18 moderate, and 16 difficult routes available. The routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Noáin (Valle De Elorz)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Noáin (Valle De Elorz) offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 35 routes available. This includes 4 easy, 18 moderate, and 16 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The terrain in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is diverse, characterized by mountain ranges like the Sierra de Tajonar and Sierra de Alaiz, which provide varied inclines and descents. You'll also find lush landscapes along the Elorz River and shaded trails through ancient forests, including beech, oak, and kermes oak groves.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy mountain bike routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) that are suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails offer a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Noáin (Valle De Elorz) features 16 difficult routes. These trails often include significant elevation gains and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988245903/castillo-de-irulegui-circular-por-la-sierra-de-tajonar-y-balsa-eskoriz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Irulegui – circular por la Sierra de Tajonar y Balsa Eskoriz&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses the Sierra de Tajonar.

Q: Can I find family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy and moderate routes that are suitable for families. These trails often follow river paths or less strenuous forest tracks, allowing for an enjoyable outing for all ages. Consider exploring some of the 4 easy routes available.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) dog-friendly?
A: Generally, mountain bike trails in natural areas like Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to longer rides and you keep them under control. It's always a good idea to check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter several notable landmarks and natural features. These include the impressive 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zokorena Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts remnants of ancient forests and the picturesque Elorz River.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24667095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezkoriz Pond – View of the Elorz Valley loop from Noain-Aeropuerto / Aireportua&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views and returns to its origin.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The mountain biking experience in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from challenging mountain ascents to serene riverside paths, and the stunning views of the Elorz Valley.

Q: Is there public transport access to the mountain bike trails?
A: Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is well-connected, and while specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, the region's proximity to Pamplona and its role on the Camino de Santiago suggest reasonable access. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to villages near trail starting points.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in Valle De Elorz?
A: Generally, permits are not required for mountain biking on established trails in the Valle De Elorz. However, it's always good practice to respect private land, follow local regulations, and stay on marked paths, especially in areas that might be part of the Camino de Santiago or protected natural spaces.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can also be suitable, especially in the shaded forest areas, but be mindful of higher temperatures. Winter rides are possible but may encounter muddy or slippery conditions, particularly on rugged sections.
tips: To get to the top you can choose a wide variety of paths, each one with its technical difficulty but surely with any of them you will enjoy the journey and especially the views. Coming from Elorz, the GR 220 path is impassable in sections, I do not recommend going along it, besides that it is a path for walkers and it can be somewhat crowded.
Ruins found by a miracle I never imagined finding something like this in the middle of nowhere ... (they have been marked).

Nice start through forest. Then some big rut.
Fun dry.
Beautiful views from this viewpoint that is located on the side of the path, somewhat hidden but that can be easily seen.

Irulegi Castle located in the Sierra de Tajonar is a very picturesque place that was once strategic to defend the city of Pamplona from the Moorish sieges. You can go around the hill along a tree-lined path, a very pleasant journey ideal to enjoy with the family.
Incredible views, a lot of tranquility and a lot of peace, highly recommended to disconnect
